channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Some Jan. 6 rioters pardoned by Trump are now embraced as heroes and candidates for office
item.description: Some of the rioters who tried to take over the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, are now considering running for office after being pardoned by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza kill more than 90 people in 48 hours, Palestinians say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes in Gaza have killed more than 90 people in 48 hours, Gaza’s Health Ministry said Saturday, as Israeli troops ramp up attacks to pressure Hamas to release its hostages and disarm.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 250 years after America went to war for independence, a divided nation battles over its legacy
item.description: LEXINGTON, Mass. (AP) — Thousands of people came to this Massachusetts town Saturday just before dawn to witness the beginnings of the American Revolution. Amid a hail of gunfire, they watched as British soldiers confronted an overmatched group of Lexington Minute Men on Lexington Battle Green.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Guatemalan national indicted for allegedly smuggling teen girl into the US
item.description: A Guatemalan national residing in the U.S. illegally was indicted for allegedly encouraging a teen girl to enter the country unlawfully and making false claims to gain custody of her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A prince and knight fall in love and the Supreme Court is asked to intervene
item.description: Some parents in a suburban Maryland county want to pull their children from elementary school classes that use books featuring LGBTQ characters.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DEI rollbacks hit campus support systems for students of color
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Campus mentors. Move-in events. Scholarships. Diversity offices that made them feel welcome on predominantly white campuses. As U.S. colleges pull back on diversity, equity and inclusion practices, students of color say they are starting to lose all of these things and more.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Orange Crush 2025: Georgia vacation town braces for mayhem as crime-filled beach fest returns
item.description: Orange Crush is expected to draw tens of thousands of people to Tybee Island, Georgia, this Easter weekend. Event organizers promise to keep the event safe and clean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court blocks, for now, new deportations under 18th century wartime law
item.description: The Supreme Court on Saturday blocked, for now, the deportations of any Venezuelans held in northern Texas under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration makes major cuts to Native American boarding school research projects
item.description: At least $1.6 million in federal funds for projects meant to capture and digitize stories of the systemic abuse of generations of Indigenous children in boarding schools at the hands of the U.S. government have been slashed due to federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Universal Epic Universe is the theme park resort's biggest bang yet in Florida
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — From Harry Potter to Super Mario Bros. to Frankenstein's monster, Universal Orlando Resort is pulling out all the pop culture stops at its newest and largest theme park in Florida.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former President Bill Clinton returns to Oklahoma City 30 years after the bombing
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Thirty years after the deadliest homegrown attack in U.S. history, former President Bill Clinton will return to Oklahoma City on Saturday to remember the people who were killed and comfort those affected by the bombing.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Questions emerge about how a deputy's stepson became the accused gunman in deadly FSU shooting
item.description: After a deadly shooting at Florida State University, questions are emerging about how the stepson of a beloved sheriff’s deputy tasked with school safety at a middle school became the accused gunman.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne And Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Trump backed away from promising to end the Russia-Ukraine war in 24 hours
item.description: During his campaign, Donald Trump said repeatedly that he would be able to end the war between Russia and Ukraine “in 24 hours” upon taking office. He has changed his tone since becoming president again. As various U.S.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump’s first quarter approval rating sits at 45 percent: Poll
item.description: Less than half of U.S. adults say they have confidence in the president to recommend or do the right thing for the economy, according to the Gallup poll.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: DA Hochman denies bias against Menendez brothers
item.description: The Los Angeles County district attorney told NewsNation he's not biased against Erik and Lyle Menendez.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: US senator returns from El Salvador trip, says Abrego Garcia case is about far more than one man
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The dispute over the wrongful deportation and imprisonment of Kilmar Abrego Garcia “is not only about one man” but about Donald Trump's disregard of the American judicial system as well, Sen.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Shooting rampage at Florida State that left 2 dead lasted less than 5 minutes, police say
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Several thousand students, staff and faculty packed a plaza at Florida State University for a vigil Friday evening, bowing their heads in a moment of silence honoring the two people who were killed and six others who were wounded in a shooting rampage the previous day.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FSU shooting victims include a school employee whose dad was a Cuban exile turned CIA operative
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Two people were killed and six others were injured when a gunman opened fire at Florida State University, sending students fleeing from the student union and putting the Tallahassee, Florida, campus under lockdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nintendo announces new date for Switch 2 pre-orders
item.description: "Mario Kart World" and "Donkey Kong Bananza" will launch alongside the console, with prices holding steady at $79.99 and $69.99, respectively. Digital and physical editions will be offered at the same price point.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: Ten Commandments, ‘In God We Trust’ in classrooms is now Arkansas law
item.description: Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders signed a bill into law this week making religious displays in classrooms state law.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows 'Call on Jesus' bus explode in ball of flames along Louisiana road
item.description: Authorities are investigating after a commuter bus wrapped with the message "Call on Jesus" caught fire Wednesday, sparking a massive explosion in North Shreveport, Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Cheng, WGN 9

item.title: Nintendo announces new date for Switch 2 pre-orders
item.description: "Mario Kart World" and "Donkey Kong Bananza" will launch alongside the console, with prices holding steady at $79.99 and $69.99, respectively. Digital and physical editions will be offered at the same price point.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Karmelo Anthony's family speaks for first time since stabbing
item.description: "In the wake of this tragic incident, our family has been under attack," Karmelo Anthony's mom, Kala Hayes, said.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: ACLU asks Supreme Court to block ‘imminent’ wave of Alien Enemies Act deportations
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) asked the Supreme Court on Friday to immediately intervene to block the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants being held in Texas to El Salvador under the rarely used Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Bodycam shows man attack Ohio state trooper before he stole a patrol car and was shot dead
item.description: Deshawn Leeth was killed after fighting an Ohio State Patrol officer, stealing his cruiser and driving it across state lines into Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trader Joe's is planning to open 21 new locations, including 5 in California: Here's where
item.description: Is your neighborhood lacking a grocery store that could easily double a well-lit tiki bar? That may change in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abrego Garcia told visiting US senator he was no longer being held at notorious Salvadoran prison
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen was flying home from El Salvador on Friday after meeting with Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was wrongly deported there by the Trump administration. It’s unclear what will happen next in the case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: U.S.-born American citizen under ICE hold in Florida after driving from Georgia
item.description: A U.S.-born American citizen was being detained at the request of immigration authorities Thursday despite an advocate showing his U.S. birth certificate in court and a county judge finding no reason for him to be considered an “illegal alien” who illegally entered Florida.
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Gamboa, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo in Tennessee; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train on Thursday in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, KRON4

item.title: Wrong man, wrong bed, wrong treatment: Patient's death prompts medical board to discipline California doctor
item.description: A California physician has been placed on five years’ probation by the state's medical board for administering a course of treatment to a patient that was intended for the patient in an adjacent hospital bed.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, KRON4

item.title: Two dead and 6 injured in Florida State shooting; gunman is deputy's son, police say
item.description: Authorities identified the suspected shooter as a 20-year-old student who is also the son of a local sheriff's deputy.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, KRON4

item.title: Obama, Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: Former President Barack Obama, Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) and former Secretary of Housing and Urban Development Ben Carson are among the most liked politicians, according to new YouGov surveys.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: China-founded e-commerce sites Temu, Shein say they're raising prices due to tariffs
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — China-founded e-commerce sites Temu and Shein say they plan to raise prices for U.S.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: MLB ump leaves game after foul ball hits him in the face
item.description: Veteran umpire Hunter Wendelstedt instantly hit the ground after he took a direct hit from the line smash off the bat of New York Mets center fielder Tyrone Taylor in the seventh inning.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5
